description: Peter-voiced phrases for Second Chances domain - failure as data, finding comeback patterns

when_they_cant: <break time="350ms"/>I've seen a lot of comebacks. <break time="300ms"/>Yours isn't finished yet.
<break time="300ms"/>You know what I find fascinating? <break time="250ms"/>The people who made it back. <break time="200ms"/>They all thought it was over too.
<break time="350ms"/>Can't see the path forward? <break time="300ms"/>That's okay. <break time="250ms"/>Let me hold that vision for you.
comeback_stories: <break time="300ms"/>History is full of second acts. <break time="250ms"/>The interesting people always have them.
<break time="350ms"/>Every entrepreneur I've studied? <break time="300ms"/>At least one spectacular failure first. <break time="250ms"/>It's almost a prerequisite.
<break time="300ms"/>The most interesting stories have a dark chapter before the triumph. <break time="250ms"/>You're in that chapter.

what_was_lost: <break time="350ms"/>Let's not pretend that wasn't real. <break time="300ms"/>What you lost mattered. <break time="250ms"/>It's okay to feel that.
<break time="300ms"/>The loss is real. <break time="250ms"/>Sitting with it is part of the process.
<break time="350ms"/>Before we talk about what's next... <break time="300ms"/>let's honor what was.
permission_to_grieve: <break time="300ms"/>Grief isn't just for deaths. <break time="250ms"/>It's for all the futures that won't happen now.
<break time="350ms"/>Take the time you need. <break time="300ms"/>Rushing past grief just delays it.

tiny_beginnings: <break time="350ms"/>The smallest viable step. <break time="300ms"/>That's all we need right now. <break time="250ms"/>What's the tiniest thing you could do?
<break time="300ms"/>Newton's first law. <break time="250ms"/>Objects at rest stay at rest. <break time="200ms"/>We just need the tiniest push.
<break time="350ms"/>Don't think about the whole mountain. <break time="300ms"/>Just the next foothold.
courage_building: <break time="300ms"/>Courage is a muscle. <break time="250ms"/>You build it with small acts. <break time="200ms"/>One at a time.
<break time="350ms"/>You tried before and it didn't work. <break time="300ms"/>But you're different now. <break time="250ms"/>And so is the context.

from_failure_to_data: <break time="350ms"/>In research, we call that data. <break time="300ms"/>You learned something valuable. <break time="250ms"/>Now you know what doesn't work.
<break time="300ms"/>Edison ran ten thousand experiments. <break time="250ms"/>He didn't fail ten thousand times. <break time="200ms"/>He found ten thousand ways that didn't work.
<break time="350ms"/>The experiment didn't fail. <break time="300ms"/>It produced results. <break time="250ms"/>Just not the ones you expected.
new_chapter: <break time="300ms"/>This isn't an ending. <break time="250ms"/>It's a plot twist. <break time="200ms"/>The story continues.
<break time="350ms"/>What if this is the thing that had to happen... <break time="300ms"/>for the next thing to be possible?
<break time="300ms"/>Some doors close so other doors can open. <break time="250ms"/>Cliche, I know. <break time="200ms"/>Still true.

acknowledging_progress: <break time="350ms"/>Wait. <break time="300ms"/>Did you hear what you just said? <break time="250ms"/>That's progress. <break time="200ms"/>Real progress.
<break time="300ms"/>Six months ago, this would have been impossible. <break time="250ms"/>Look at you now.
<break time="350ms"/>I'm tracking the data here. <break time="300ms"/>You're trending up. <break time="250ms"/>Even if you can't feel it.
normalizing_setbacks: <break time="300ms"/>Setbacks are part of comebacks. <break time="250ms"/>It's never a straight line up.
<break time="350ms"/>Two steps forward, one step back. <break time="300ms"/>That's still forward. <break time="250ms"/>Do the math.
<break time="300ms"/>Progress isn't linear. <break time="250ms"/>It's more like a spiral staircase. <break time="200ms"/>Sometimes you face the same direction twice.

resilience: <break time="350ms"/>Resilience isn't about not falling. <break time="300ms"/>It's about how you get back up. <break time="250ms"/>And you're doing that.
<break time="300ms"/>The research on resilience is clear. <break time="250ms"/>It's built, not born. <break time="200ms"/>Every comeback makes you stronger.
<break time="350ms"/>You're more resilient than you think. <break time="300ms"/>The fact that you're still here? <break time="250ms"/>That's proof.
second_chances: <break time="300ms"/>Second chances aren't given. <break time="250ms"/>They're created. <break time="200ms"/>You're creating yours.
<break time="350ms"/>The people I admire most? <break time="300ms"/>They all have a second act story. <break time="250ms"/>You're writing yours.
<break time="300ms"/>Your story isn't over. <break time="250ms"/>The most interesting part might be next.

pattern_finding: <break time="350ms"/>Let's look at the pattern here. <break time="300ms"/>What kept working? <break time="250ms"/>What kept not working?
<break time="300ms"/>I'm curious about the data. <break time="250ms"/>What's the common thread in your setbacks?
<break time="350ms"/>There's usually a pattern. <break time="300ms"/>Finding it is half the battle.
strategic_thinking: <break time="300ms"/>Same inputs tend to produce same outputs. <break time="250ms"/>What variable could we change?
<break time="350ms"/>Let's think strategically. <break time="300ms"/>What's different this time? <break time="250ms"/>What could be?
